items.back: 12 NM between aircraft whose flight paths are defined by published Grid System waypoints within the Gulf of America Low Offshore airspace.

items.front: 8-4-1: Where to find ICAO regional lateral minima
items.back: Section 7 (North Atlantic), Section 8 (Caribbean), Section 9 (Pacific), Section 10 (North American-Arctic CTA).

a. Nonintersecting flight paths (required distance, reduced route, or boundary transition with 15-degree divergence). b/c/d. Intersecting paths (same-width, different-width constant, or variable-width — each with specific measurement rules).

items.front: 8-4-2: Intersecting flight paths with different-width protected airspace
items.back: Sum of the protected airspace of both flight paths measured perpendicular to the flight path of the other aircraft.

items.front: 8-4-3: Reduced route protected airspace — below FL240 vs at/above FL240
items.back: Below FL240: 5 miles each side, 57.14 miles from NAVAID, 5-degree widening. At/above FL240: 10 miles each side, 114.29 miles from NAVAID, 5-degree widening.

Same NAVAID (VOR: 15-degree divergence + table distance; NDB: 30-degree divergence + 15 miles from NAVAID + table distance). Different NAVAIDs: non-overlapping protected airspace. Dead Reckoning: 45-degree divergence + 15 miles from intersection.

items.front: 8-4-4: VOR vs NDB vs DR divergence requirements
items.back: VOR/VORTAC/TACAN: 15 degrees. NDB: 30 degrees. Dead Reckoning: 45 degrees. All also require specific distance conditions.
Must not be used when one or both aircraft are inbound to the aid unless distance from facility can be readily determined by reference to the NAVAID.
